body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This is a strong differentiator if we keep it private-draft-first: anchored feedback on shared previews gives writers actionable revision input without turning the product into a public social platform. That positions us between Living Writer sharing and Inkitt community engagement, with a tighter workflow for serious draft iteration.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer supports sharing manuscripts/chapters via links and invitations with permission levels (view/comment/edit), but the docs emphasize sharing mechanics over an anchored feedback triage workflow: https://guides.livingwriter.com/share-manuscript-and-chapters
- Living Writer’s own update notes indicate a feedback system is still being launched and collaboration comments are evolving, which leaves room for us to define a clearer writer-centric feedback inbox now: https://guides.livingwriter.com/whats-new/changelog/june-17-2025
- Inkitt strongly centers reader interaction through reviews and chapter comments/emojis in a community context; our opportunity is to deliver high-signal private beta feedback instead of engagement-first social loops: https://www.inkitt.com/writersblog/why-subscribe/
- Campfire highlights collaboration (invite collaborators to view/comment/edit), but broad co-writing and community positioning can be noisier than a focused beta-reader review lane for manuscript revision: https://www.campfirewriting.com/ and https://www.campfirewriting.com/pricing

## Risks / trade-offs
- Comment moderation/spam and abuse controls become necessary once external readers can comment.
- Anchoring comments to mutable text can create orphaned references after edits unless we define robust anchoring behavior.
- Scope can bloat into full collaboration if boundaries (no real-time co-authoring/public feed) are not enforced.

## Recommendation
Approve with a positioning constraint: optimize for private, high-signal revision loops (anchor + resolve + writer inbox) and explicitly avoid community/discovery mechanics in v1. Add acceptance language for anchor persistence behavior after manuscript edits to prevent UX regressions.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Review Request

Requesting Market Research validation for differentiation and scope filter before engineering intake.

- Ticket: [BIN-71](/BIN/issues/BIN-71)
- Goal linkage: privacy-first trust layer for optional AI in drafting workflow
- Ask: approve/reframe with competitor-backed positioning

Assign to: Market Research
